nodes.task:
Implement the foundational scorer/gates/metric core. SCOPE: only NEW files and ADDITIVE changes to src/hone/grader.py and tests/test_grader.py. Do NOT modify src/hone/repo_frontier.py or src/hone/cli.py — that is owned by node `cli-config-integration`.

Files to touch:
- src/hone/grader.py [MODIFY]: keep `run_grader(grader, prompt_path, timeout_seconds)` signature and existing stdout-last-float fallback. Add to GraderResult: `raw_score: float`, `metrics: dict[str, float]` (default {}), `tasks: list[dict]` (default []), `gate_rejected: bool = False`, `gate_failures: list[str] = []`, `result_json: dict | None = None`. Implementation: before invoking grader, allocate a tempfile path and pass it via env var `HONE_RESULT_PATH`. Also export `HONE_WORKDIR` env var pointing at `prompt_path`. After the subprocess, if HONE_RESULT_PATH file exists and is valid JSON, parse `score` (float), `metrics` (dict), `tasks` (list) from it and prefer those over stdout. If the JSON is missing/invalid, fall back to the stdout-last-float behaviour unchanged. raw_score = the parsed/parsed-stdout value; `score` field stays the same as today (raw float — normalization is the metric module's job). Do NOT introduce gate_rejected logic into run_grader; gates are evaluated separately. Existing five tests in tests/test_grader.py MUST keep passing.
- src/hone/gates.py [CREATE]: dataclass `Gate(name: str, command: Path)` and `GateResult(gate: Gate, passed: bool, returncode: int, stderr: str)`. Function `run_gates(gates: list[Gate], workdir: Path, timeout_seconds: int = 600) -> list[GateResult]` that runs each gate as `<command> <workdir>` (same protocol as scorer); nonzero returncode => passed=False. Stop short-circuit allowed but not required. Function `any_gate_rejected(results: list[GateResult]) -> bool`. Keep the module under ~120 lines. No env-var protocol — stderr is the diagnostic surface like grader.
- src/hone/metric.py [CREATE]: `Direction = Literal["max", "min"]`. `normalize_utility(raw_score: float, direction: Direction) -> float` returns `raw_score` if direction==max else `-raw_score` (so bigger utility is always better). `is_stalled(utilities: list[float], best_utility: float, stall_after: int) -> bool` — returns True iff stall_after > 0 and the last `stall_after` utilities are all <= best_utility (no strict improvement within window). Module under 60 lines.
- tests/test_grader.py [MODIFY]: ADD tests (do not delete existing). (a) rich-JSON path: grader writes `{"score": 0.7, "metrics": {"a": 1}, "tasks": [{"id":"t1","score":1}]}` to $HONE_RESULT_PATH and prints noise to stdout — assert result.score==0.7, result.metrics=={"a":1}, result.tasks has one element. (b) malformed-JSON falls back to stdout float. (c) missing-JSON falls back to stdout float. (d) HONE_WORKDIR env exported and equals prompt_path.
- tests/test_gates.py [CREATE]: passing gate (exit 0) -> passed=True; failing gate (exit 1) -> passed=False with stderr captured; multi-gate run preserves order; any_gate_rejected works.
- tests/test_metric.py [CREATE]: normalize_utility max/min directions; is_stalled True/False boundary cases including stall_after=0 (always False) and an empty history.

Commands to run before signaling pass: `uv run pytest tests/test_grader.py tests/test_gates.py tests/test_metric.py -q`. All existing tests in the repo MUST also still pass: `uv run pytest -q`. Do NOT introduce a dependency on `gepa`. Do NOT touch repo_frontier.py, cli.py, storage.py, policy.py, mutators/.

Write a one-page coder.md handoff at the worktree root with: (1) summary of new public API (`GraderResult` shape, `Gate`, `run_gates`, `normalize_utility`, `is_stalled`); (2) integration notes for the next node (`cli-config-integration`) covering exact import paths, expected wiring sequence (gate -> scorer -> normalize -> stall-check), and which fields belong on RunConfig.

flt workflow pass when tests are green and your coder.md handoff is written.

Wire the scorer/gates/metric core into the loop and CLI; introduce project-mode UX. SCOPE: src/hone/repo_frontier.py, src/hone/cli.py, src/hone/config.py (new), src/hone/storage.py (small additions only). Do NOT touch src/hone/grader.py, src/hone/gates.py, src/hone/metric.py — they were finalized in `scorer-gates-core`; import from them.

Files to touch:
- src/hone/config.py [CREATE]: dataclass `RunConfig(src_dir: Path, scorer: Path, mutator: str = "harness:claude-code:sonnet", gates: list[Path] = [], budget: int = 20, metric_direction: Literal["max","min"] = "max", stall_after: int = 0, frontier_size: int = 4, objective: str = ..., scorer_timeout: int = 3600, gate_timeout: int = 600, output: Path | None = None, policy_dir: Path | None = None, ace_interval: int = 0, ace_model: str = "")`. Functions `load_config(path: Path) -> RunConfig` (TOML via stdlib `tomllib`) and `write_default_config(path: Path, src_dir: Path, scorer: Path)`. All paths are stored relative to the config file's directory and resolved at load time. Default config location: `./.hone/config.toml`.
- src/hone/cli.py [MODIFY]:
  * Add new option family on `run`: `--scorer` (Path, exists=True) as primary, with `--grader` kept as a deprecated alias that emits a `console.print('[yellow]--grader is deprecated; use --scorer[/yellow]')` warning and sets the same value. Mutually exclusive at runtime — error if both passed. Existing `hone run --dir X --grader ./g.sh` MUST still work unchanged.
  * Add `--gate` (multi-value, repeatable, Path exists=True) on `run` and `optimize`.
  * Add `--metric-direction` (Choice["max","min"], default "max"), `--stall-after` (int, default 0, min=0), `--scorer-timeout` (alias of existing --grader-timeout, with --grader-timeout deprecated), `--gate-timeout` (default 600).
  * Add `init` command: `hone init [--dir SRC] [--scorer PATH] [--config PATH=./.hone/config.toml]`. Writes a default `.hone/config.toml` next to the cwd; scaffolds it with required fields and commented examples for gates/metric_direction/stall_after.
  * Add `optimize` command: `hone optimize [--config PATH=./.hone/config.toml] [--budget N override] [--output DIR override]`. Loads RunConfig, then calls the same code path as `run`. The `run` command becomes thin sugar over RunConfig+optimize when invoked one-shot.
  * Add a `hone report --run RUN_DIR --output FILE.md` stub that imports `hone.report` and calls into it (the actual implementation lives in node `static-report`; here just leave a one-liner that imports lazily inside the function and emits a clear ImportError-handled message if `hone.report` is missing — but the import path MUST be `from hone.report import write_report`). The `static-report` node will satisfy that import.
- src/hone/repo_frontier.py [MODIFY]:
  * Extend `optimize_repo_frontier` signature with: `gates: list[Path] = ()`, `metric_direction: Literal["max","min"] = "max"`, `stall_after: int = 0`, `gate_timeout_seconds: int = 600`. (Keep all existing kwargs for backwards compatibility.)
  * Per iteration: AFTER the mutator commit and BEFORE `run_grader`, run gates via `hone.gates.run_gates(gate_objs, workdir, gate_timeout_seconds)`. If any gate rejects: record an attempt with `accepted=False, error="gate:<name>"`, append a mutations.jsonl row with `gate_failures: [...]`, do NOT call the scorer, reset to parent, delete the child branch, continue.
  * Compute utility via `normalize_utility(raw_score, metric_direction)`. Store `child.utility` alongside score (extend RepoCandidate dataclass with `utility: float = 0.0` and a `gate_failures: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)` field). Use UTILITY (not raw score) for `best` selection and `_update_frontier` ranking. Display still shows raw `score` to user.
  * Stall stopping: maintain `iters_since_best_improved` counter; reset to 0 when child.utility > best.utility; otherwise increment. After updating best, if `stall_after > 0 and iters_since_best_improved >= stall_after`: emit `[stall] no utility improvement in {stall_after} iters; stopping early`, break loop. Set `manifest.status = "stalled"` in that case.
  * Persist `gate_failures` and `utility` and `metric_direction` into mutations.jsonl rows and run.json.
- src/hone/storage.py [MODIFY]: extend `RunManifest` with `metric_direction: str = "max"`, `stall_after: int = 0`, `stalled: bool = False`. Keep backwards compatibility for loading old manifests (use defaults for missing fields when calling `RunManifest(**data)` — wrap in try/except KeyError or filter to known field names).
- tests/test_config.py [CREATE]: roundtrip RunConfig <-> TOML, defaults applied for missing fields, relative paths resolved against config dir, error on missing `src_dir`.
- tests/test_cli.py [CREATE]: typer.testing.CliRunner smoke. (a) `hone init` writes a TOML file with required keys. (b) `hone run --dir X --grader ./g.sh` still parses. (c) `hone run --grader X --scorer Y` errors. (d) `--metric-direction min` with a grader that returns 0.5 results in best.utility == -0.5 (use a tiny in-process stub mutator). Mark slow tests with `@pytest.mark.slow` or skip if real mutator would be invoked.
- tests/test_repo_frontier.py [MODIFY]: keep existing two tests passing. ADD a stall-stopping test (synthetic mutator that returns flat scores; assert run terminates before budget) and a gate-rejection test (gate that always fails; assert no scorer call, attempt recorded with gate_failures).

Commands to run before signaling pass: `uv run pytest -q` — all 20 existing tests + new tests must be green. Do NOT add `gepa` dep. Do NOT push or open PRs.

Write a one-page coder.md handoff at the worktree root with: (1) the new RunConfig schema; (2) the new CLI surface (`hone run`, `hone init`, `hone optimize`, `hone report` stub); (3) the new mutations.jsonl row shape (added utility + gate_failures + metric_direction); (4) integration points the report/discover/examples nodes will read.

flt workflow pass when tests are green and your coder.md handoff is written.

Implement the static report generator. SCOPE: src/hone/report.py (new), src/hone/cli.py (only the body of the `hone report` subcommand which the previous node stubbed — wire it to call into `hone.report.write_report`), tests/test_report.py (new). Do NOT modify grader.py, gates.py, metric.py, repo_frontier.py, config.py, storage.py beyond reading them.

Files to touch:
- src/hone/report.py [CREATE]:
  * `read_run(run_dir: Path) -> RunData` — parse `run.json` (RunManifest), `mutations.jsonl` (one row per iter), and optional `traces/iter-XXX.json`. Tolerate older runs missing utility/gate_failures fields (default them).
  * `render_markdown(data: RunData) -> str` — sections: Header (run_id, created_at, mutator_spec, budget, metric_direction, stall_after, status); Best candidate (idx, sha[:12], score, utility, branch); Score trend (a small ASCII sparkline or a per-iter table of iter|parent|child|score|utility|delta|kept); Frontier (final indices); Changed files for best (list from base diff); Gate failures table (iter|gate names|stderr first line); Footer with run_dir.
  * `render_html(data: RunData) -> str` — same content, simple inline-styled HTML with a `<table>` for the iteration log; no JS, no external CSS.
  * `write_report(run_dir: Path, output: Path, format: Literal["md","html","auto"] = "auto")` — `auto` chooses by `output.suffix` (.html -> html, else markdown). Creates parent dirs.
  * Keep module under ~250 lines. Pure stdlib — no jinja, no markdown libs.
- src/hone/cli.py [MODIFY]: implement the body of `report` (the previous node left a stub that imports `from hone.report import write_report`). Add `--format` option (default `auto`). Print the resolved output path on success.
- tests/test_report.py [CREATE]:
  * Build a synthetic run dir in tmp_path with run.json + mutations.jsonl (seed + 3 iters: one improvement, one regression, one gate-rejected). Call `write_report` for both md and html. Assert key strings are present in the rendered output: best score, the iteration count, the gate-rejected entry, the changed files list. Assert html contains `<table` and `</html>`. Assert auto-format picks the right one by suffix.
  * Smoke-test the CLI via `typer.testing.CliRunner`: `hone report --run <tmp_run> --output <tmp.md>` exits 0 and writes the file.

Commands to run before signaling pass: `uv run pytest tests/test_report.py -q` and `uv run pytest -q` for the full suite. Live dashboards are out of scope — static rendering only.

Write a one-page coder.md handoff at the worktree root listing the supported output formats, the data fields the report relies on (so future schema changes know what they break), and any TODOs (e.g. score-trend ASCII chart improvements) deferred to follow-up.

flt workflow pass when tests are green and your coder.md handoff is written.

Add the `hone discover` skeleton + docs. SCOPE: src/hone/discover.py (new), src/hone/cli.py (add the `discover` subcommand only — do not touch other commands), docs/discover.md (new). NO real autonomous benchmark construction. Skeleton + docs only.

Files to touch:
- src/hone/discover.py [CREATE]:
  * Module docstring describes the planned UX: `hone discover --task <natural-language>` -> proposes a skeleton scorer.sh + gate.sh + a starter src/ tree + a `.hone/config.toml` for the user to iterate on.
  * Dataclasses: `DiscoverSpec(task_description: str, target_dir: Path)`, `DiscoverPlan(scorer_path: Path, gates: list[Path], starter_files: dict[Path, str], config_toml: str, rationale: str)`.
  * Functions (all skeletons):
    - `propose_plan(spec: DiscoverSpec, mutator_spec: str) -> DiscoverPlan`: raises `NotImplementedError("hone discover: planning not implemented in v1 final-form — see docs/discover.md")`.
    - `materialize_plan(plan: DiscoverPlan)`: also `NotImplementedError`.
  * Each function has a 5-15 line docstring describing exact intended behavior, the inputs/outputs, and pointers to which other module would consume them. Do NOT add a dependency on `gepa`.
- src/hone/cli.py [MODIFY]: add `hone discover --task TEXT [--target DIR=.] [--mutator SPEC]` subcommand. Calls `propose_plan` inside a try/except NotImplementedError; on the catch, prints a clear panel: "hone discover is a v1 final-form skeleton — see docs/discover.md for the planned UX. Configure manually with `hone init` for now." Returns exit code 0 (informational, not an error). Do NOT touch the `run`, `init`, `optimize`, `report`, `version`, `reflect` commands.
- docs/discover.md [CREATE]: ~150-300 lines. Sections: "Why discover", "Inputs/Outputs" (DiscoverSpec, DiscoverPlan), "Planned UX walkthrough" (concrete example: a user types `hone discover --task 'optimize a sorting function for speed'` and what the system would produce), "Why it's deferred" (explicitly: skeleton-only in this release; full autonomous benchmark construction is out of medium-scope per the user's binding decisions), "How a contributor could implement it" (rough algorithm referencing `propose_plan`/`materialize_plan`).

Tests: add ONE tiny test in tests/test_discover.py asserting: (a) `propose_plan` raises NotImplementedError; (b) the CLI command exits 0 and the output mentions "docs/discover.md".

Commands to run before signaling pass: `uv run pytest tests/test_discover.py -q` and `uv run pytest -q` for the full suite.

Write a one-page coder.md handoff at the worktree root summarizing the skeleton API surface and the explicit NON-goals so future work doesn't accidentally creep into autonomous benchmark construction.

flt workflow pass when tests are green and your coder.md handoff is written.

Add example scorers/gates and a tiny end-to-end smoke test. SCOPE: examples/ (new files only — do NOT modify existing examples/simple-grader.sh or simple-prompt.md), tests/test_e2e_smoke.py (new). Do NOT touch src/hone/.

Files to touch:
- examples/rich-scorer.sh [CREATE]: bash script that demonstrates the new $HONE_RESULT_PATH protocol. Reads optional `$HONE_WORKDIR`. Counts `keyword` mentions like simple-grader.sh, but also writes a JSON file to `$HONE_RESULT_PATH` containing `{"score": <0..1>, "metrics": {"keyword_count": N, "length_chars": L}, "tasks": [{"id":"keyword-density","score":<0..1>}]}`. Still echoes the score on stdout for legacy compatibility. Mark executable in the file header comment (the runner will chmod). Include a top comment showing how to invoke it.
- examples/gate-shellcheck.sh [CREATE]: invokes `shellcheck` against any *.sh files under the workdir; nonzero exit if any errors. If shellcheck isn't installed, exit 0 with a warning to stderr. Demonstrates a real gate.
- examples/gate-noop.sh [CREATE]: trivial pass-through gate (always exit 0) for tests/docs.
- examples/.hone/config.toml [CREATE]: sample project config showing src_dir, scorer (rich-scorer.sh), gates = [gate-noop.sh], metric_direction = "max", stall_after = 5, budget = 10, mutator = "harness:claude-code:sonnet". Include comments explaining each field.
- examples/README.md [CREATE if missing, else MODIFY]: short table of contents linking simple-grader.sh, rich-scorer.sh, gate-*.sh, and explaining when to use each.
- tests/test_e2e_smoke.py [CREATE]: end-to-end test that does NOT invoke any real LLM mutator. Strategy: use typer.testing.CliRunner + monkeypatch `hone.repo_frontier.optimize_repo_frontier` (or build a tiny in-process StubMutator and register it via the mutators registry — pick whichever existing test-utility pattern the repo already uses; check tests/test_mutators.py and tests/test_repo_frontier.py for the established pattern). The test must:
  (1) Create a tmp src dir with a stub file. (2) Use examples/rich-scorer.sh as the scorer (chmod +x). (3) Use examples/gate-noop.sh as a gate. (4) Run `hone run --dir tmp --scorer rich-scorer.sh --gate gate-noop.sh --budget 1 --metric-direction max --output tmp_out` (with the mutator stubbed so it does a single trivial edit). (5) Assert: run.json exists, mutations.jsonl has >=2 lines (seed + 1 iter), traces/iter-001.json exists, the gate result is recorded in the iter-1 row, the seed score came from the rich JSON (metrics dict in the trace).
  Mark with `@pytest.mark.slow` if it takes >2 s; otherwise leave it in the default suite. SKIP gracefully (`pytest.skip`) on platforms where bash is unavailable.

Commands to run before signaling pass: `uv run pytest tests/test_e2e_smoke.py -q` and `uv run pytest -q` for the full suite.

Write a one-page coder.md handoff at the worktree root listing the example scripts and what each demonstrates, plus the stubbing strategy used in the e2e test (so future contributors don't think the test is invoking a real LLM).

flt workflow pass when tests are green and your coder.md handoff is written.
